verses.commentary: Do not let your hearts be troubled. You believe in God; believe also in Me. Command against anxiety. Trust in God extends to trust in Jesus. Equal faith. Divinity implied.

My Father's house has many rooms; if that were not so, would I have told you that I am going there to prepare a place for you? Heaven as dwelling. Monai (μοναί), rooms or dwelling places. Abundant space. Jesus prepares. Personal preparation for personal presence.

And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come back and take you to be with me that you also may be where I am. Promise of return. Second coming. Purpose: reunion. Being with Jesus is heaven's essence.
You know the way to the place where I am going. Assumption of knowledge. Jesus thinks they understand. But they don't.
Thomas said to Him, Lord, we don't know where You are going, so how can we know the way? Honest confusion. Thomas seeks clarity. Location unknown; path unknown. Reasonable objection.

Jesus answered, I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through Me. Sixth 'I AM' statement. Triple claim. Way: path to God. Truth: reality itself. Life: source of existence. Exclusive claim. Only through Jesus. No alternate routes.

If you really know Me, you will know My Father as well. From now on, you do know Him and have seen Him. Knowing Jesus means knowing Father. Present tense: they already know and have seen. Incarnation reveals Father.
Philip said, Lord, show us the Father and that will be enough for us. Philip's request. Wants theophany. Exodus 33:18 echo. Show us, and we're satisfied.

Jesus answered: Don't you know Me, Philip, even after I have been among you such a long time? Anyone who has seen Me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father? Gentle rebuke. Long association. Seeing Jesus is seeing Father. Perfect revelation. Incarnation is theophany.

Don't you believe that I am in the Father, and that the Father is in Me? The words I say to you I do not speak on My own authority. Rather, it is the Father, living in Me, who is doing His work. Mutual indwelling. Jesus in Father, Father in Jesus. Words from Father. Works from Father. Unity of action and speech.

Believe Me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in Me; or at least believe on the evidence of the works themselves. Two levels of faith. Believe the word. Or if not, believe the works. Miracles validate if words don't persuade.

Very truly I tell you, whoever believes in Me will do the works I have been doing, and they will do even greater things than these, because I am going to the Father. Believers will work. Greater things: not more powerful but more extensive. Global reach through church. Ascension enables worldwide mission.

And I will do whatever you ask in My name, so that the Father may be glorified in the Son. Prayer promise. In My name: according to character, for His purposes. Condition: Father's glory. Not blank check but aligned requests.
You may ask Me for anything in My name, and I will do it. Reiteration. Prayer to Jesus directly. Anything aligned with His name and character. Promise stands.
If you love Me, keep My commands. Love proven by obedience. Not emotional but volitional. Keeping commands demonstrates love. John 15:10, 1 John 5:3.

And I will ask the Father, and He will give you another advocate to help you and be with you forever. Promise of Paraclete. Allos paraklētos (ἄλλος παράκλητος), another of same kind. Advocate, Helper, Comforter. Permanent presence. Jesus leaves; Spirit comes.

The Spirit of truth. The world cannot accept Him, because it neither sees Him nor knows Him. But you know Him, for He lives with you and will be in you. Spirit identified. Truth's Spirit. World cannot receive. Invisibility prevents recognition. Disciples know Him. Present: with you. Future: in you. Pentecost changes preposition.
I will not leave you as orphans; I will come to you. Not abandoned. Orphanos (ὀρφανός), fatherless. Jesus returns through Spirit. Spirit is Jesus' presence.

Before long, the world will not see Me anymore, but you will see Me. Because I live, you also will live. Brief absence. Resurrection appearance. His life guarantees their life. Union with living Christ brings life.

On that day you will realize that I am in My Father, and you are in Me, and I am in you. Resurrection day brings understanding. Triple union. Jesus in Father. Believers in Jesus. Jesus in believers. Mutual indwelling completed.

Whoever has My commands and keeps them is the one who loves Me. The one who loves Me will be loved by My Father, and I too will love them and show Myself to them. Love defined by obedience. Having and keeping. Father's love follows. Jesus' love follows. Self-revelation promised. Manifestation to obedient.

Then Judas (not Judas Iscariot) said, But, Lord, why do You intend to show Yourself to us and not to the world? Judas son of James. Question about selectivity. Why private revelation? Why not public theophany?

Jesus replied, Anyone who loves Me will obey My teaching. My Father will love them, and We will come to them and make Our home with them. Condition repeated. Obedience from love. Trinitarian indwelling. We: Father and Son. Home (monē, μονή): permanent dwelling. Heaven comes to believers.

Anyone who does not love Me will not obey My teaching. These words you hear are not My own; they belong to the Father who sent Me. Opposite. No love, no obedience. Words are Father's. Rejection of Jesus' words is rejection of Father.
All this I have spoken while still with you. Present teaching. Physical presence. Face to face instruction.

But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in My name, will teach you all things and will remind you of everything I have said to you. Spirit's work. Teaching: new revelation. Reminding: recalling Jesus' words. Dual function. Illumination and memory.

Peace I leave with you; My peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid. Shalom bequest. My peace: distinctive, Jesus-sourced. Not worldly peace (temporary, external). Command against fear. Real peace in Jesus.

You heard Me say, I am going away and I am coming back to you. If you loved Me, you would be glad that I am going to the Father, for the Father is greater than I. Departure and return. Love should produce joy. Father greater: in role, not essence. Functional subordination. Incarnate humility.
I have told you now before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe. Prophecy strengthens faith. Foreknowledge proves divine authority. Fulfilled prediction validates Jesus.

I will not say much more to you, for the prince of this world is coming. He has no hold over me. Satan approaches. Gethsemane and crucifixion near. No hold: Satan has no claim. Jesus is sinless. No ground for accusation.

But he comes so that the world may learn that I love the Father and do exactly what My Father has commanded Me. Come now; let us leave. Cross demonstrates love for Father. Perfect obedience displayed. World will see. Time to move. Upper room to Gethsemane.
